sourceHighlights.title: Homebound reason
sourceHighlights.summary: Patient is not homebound as there is no evidence of special transportation needs or assistance required to leave home.

sourceHighlights.title: Medical necessity
sourceHighlights.summary: Leon Coburn, an 84-year-old male, has been referred for home health care primarily due to his diagnosis of bilateral shoulder osteoarthritis, accompanied by a history of chronic low back pain, hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, and atrial fibrillation. His recent medical evaluation indicated significant functional limitations, particularly in the range of motion of both shoulders, which is critical for daily activities such as dressing and grooming. The patient has reported pain and difficulty moving his arms, which necessitates skilled nursing and therapy interventions.

sourceHighlights.title: Prior level of function
sourceHighlights.summary: Prior level of function is not documented in the referral or chart.

sourceHighlights.title: Wound history
sourceHighlights.summary: No wound history is documented in the referral or chart.

sourceHighlights.title: Diet and fluid instructions
sourceHighlights.summary: No diet and fluid instructions are documented in the referral or chart.

sourceHighlights.title: PMH, immunizations, and DM status
sourceHighlights.summary: PMH includes arthritis, hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, atrial fibrillation, and depressive disorder. No immunizations or diabetes status is documented in the referral or chart.

sourceHighlights.title: Diagnoses and possible coding support references
sourceHighlights.summary: Primary diagnosis is bilateral shoulder osteoarthritis (M19.011, M19.012). Possible coding support references include single question alcohol screening (M19.011).

draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: Patient is not homebound as there is no evidence of special transportation needs or assistance required to leave home.

draftNarratives.label: Medical Necessity
draftNarratives.draft: Leon Coburn, an 84-year-old male, has been referred for home health care primarily due to his diagnosis of bilateral shoulder osteoarthritis, accompanied by a history of chronic low back pain, hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, and atrial fibrillation. His recent medical evaluation indicated significant functional limitations, particularly in the range of motion of both shoulders, which is critical for daily activities such as dressing and grooming. The patient has reported pain and difficulty moving his arms, which necessitates skilled nursing and therapy interventions.

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: Leon Coburn is an 84-year-old male with a history of bilateral shoulder osteoarthritis, chronic low back pain, hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, and atrial fibrillation. He has reported significant functional limitations, particularly in the range of motion of both shoulders, which affects his ability to perform daily activities such as dressing and grooming. The patient has undergone a subacromial steroid bursal injection in the right shoulder and is scheduled for follow-up in three months.

draftNarratives.label: Skilled Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Skilled interventions include physical therapy and occupational therapy to address the patient's functional limitations and improve range of motion in both shoulders.

draftNarratives.label: Plan For Next Visit
draftNarratives.draft: The plan for the next visit includes monitoring the patient's response to the subacromial steroid bursal injection, providing counseling on nonsurgical management options for shoulder arthritis, and recommending over-the-counter medications for pain relief as needed.

draftNarratives.label: Care Plan Problems / Goals / Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Problems: Bilateral shoulder osteoarthritis, chronic low back pain, hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, atrial fibrillation. Goals: Improve range of motion in both shoulders, manage pain, and maintain functional independence. Interventions: Physical therapy, occupational therapy, medication management, and patient education on arthritis management.

draftNarratives.label: Patient / Caregiver Goals
draftNarratives.draft: Patient and caregiver goals include achieving pain relief, improving shoulder mobility, and maintaining the patient's ability to perform daily activities independently.
